articles.title: Spotify expands parent-managed accounts to users on its free tier
articles.description: The feature was previously only available to paid subscribers.

articles.title: India bets billions on breaking China’s grip on smartphone manufacturing
articles.description: New Delhi announced a $6.5 billion smartphone manufacturing program and a $13.3 billion semiconductor push to deepen India's electronics supply chain.

articles.title: US charges Russian ‘bulletproof’ web hosts over cyberattacks that netted $62M from cybercrime victims
articles.description: The 2024 indictment, now unsealed, accuses three Russians and two web hosts of aiding hackers and profiting from cybercrime.

articles.title: Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation, not just models
articles.description: Anthropic-backed Ode launches as AI labs bet that embedding forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises is the key to accelerating enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: Rime picks up $24M Series A to help enterprises field customer calls
articles.description: Rime is handling over 100 million calls each month across multiple companies.

articles.title: Reelful’s AI turns your camera roll into short-form videos for social media
articles.description: The app is designed for people who want to create social content, but find traditional video editing tools too complex or time-consuming.

articles.title: Indian AI coding startup Emergent becomes a unicorn with $130M Series C
articles.description: The startup has reached a $120 million annualized revenue run rate and more than 200,000 paying customers.

articles.title: Vint Cerf is working on a plan to unleash AI agents on the open internet
articles.description: The guy behind TCP/IP is working on a standard for identifying AI agents in the wild.

articles.title: Why Realta Fusion is building a fusion reactor at an old hot dog factory
articles.description: An old Oscar Mayer factory in Wisconsin will become America's latest fusion power research and development hub.

articles.title: Backed by $60M in funding, Oak steps out of stealth to fix the identity mess that AI agents are making worse
articles.description: Co-founded by serial entrepreneur Shai Morag, Israeli identity management startup Oak is emerging out of stealth with $60 million in seed funding.

articles.title: A SpaceX vet raised $65M to pull wire harnesses out of the Cold War era
articles.description: Someone has to bundle all the wires that go into rockets, missiles, and satellites.

articles.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
articles.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

articles.title: Lorde says AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
articles.description: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said onstage.

articles.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
articles.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."

articles.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
articles.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

articles.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
articles.description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

articles.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
articles.description: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

articles.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
articles.description: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

articles.title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
articles.description: Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."

articles.title: Lucid Motors denies report it’s considering bankruptcy
articles.description: The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option.

articles.title: A most improbable astronaut just went to space
articles.description: "I pretty much, at that point in time, gave up on being an astronaut."

articles.title: How hard is it to build orbital data centers, actually?
articles.description: "The ISS radiators are expensive and heavy. We're focused on making them cheap and light."

articles.title: Sotheby's big T. rex auction raises concerns hype and wealth are upending science
articles.description: Private buyers are increasingly outbidding museums for fossils.

articles.title: Microsoft’s Secure Boot has been broken for a decade and no one noticed until now
articles.description: Old and forgotten "shims" Microsoft failed to revoke have made Secure Boot bypasses simple.

articles.title: Trump admin puts Americans in Congo on "do-not-board" list, barring return
articles.description: Citizens must now spend 21 days in a third country before they are allowed to come home.

articles.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
articles.description: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

articles.title: Probe into explosive diarrheal cases points to Taco Bell and bad lettuce
articles.description: Federal officials have not confirmed a source yet—and there may be multiple sources.

articles.title: US military sent explosive drone boats into combat for the first time
articles.description: US military’s drone boats struck an Iranian naval port as war heats up again.

articles.title: These painted e-tattoos could be the future of wearable biosensors
articles.description: Conductive ink is painted directly onto the skin in colorful custom designs, drying into working electrodes.

articles.title: Google revamps image search for its 25th anniversary with more images and more AI
articles.description: The new Google image search will use your "unique interests" to create an always-updated gallery.

articles.title: New York bans data center construction for a year, rattling AI industry
articles.description: New York’s data center moratorium may become the blueprint for anti-AI movement.

articles.title: Boomers, not Gen Z, are the generation cutting back most on alcohol
articles.description: New research overturns assumption that abstinent younger drinkers are behind weak demand.

articles.title: SpaceX is gearing up for Starship's 13th test flight later this week
articles.description: This flight will put Starship under higher pressure and test out new Starlink satellites in orbit.

articles.title: US continues to shun Ebola-infected citizens; second American sent to Germany
articles.description: The man is said to be doing well in a Frankfurt hospital.

articles.title: The US government warns that Russia state hackers are coming after your router
articles.description: With residential proxies all the rage, CISA urges router users to be vigilant.

articles.title: Ukrainian drone strikes forced Russia to stop shipping in vital sea corridor
articles.description: Ukraine’s drone blitz halted Russia’s Sea of Azov shipping in under a week.

articles.title: California creates $3,500 rebate for new electric vehicle buyers
articles.description: There's a separate $1,750 rebate for used EVs, but both rebates have a price cap.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI after ex-engineer allegedly used bug to steal trade secrets
articles.description: OpenAI accused of conspiring with former Apple employees to steal trade secrets.

articles.title: Solution to Feynman's reverse sprinkler puzzle also applies to "silly sprinklers"
articles.description: New study confirms 2024 "momentum flux theory" on how angular momentum of water flows drives rotation.

articles.title: States sue to block Paramount/WBD merger that was approved by Trump admin
articles.description: AG: Deal will bring "higher prices, lower quality, and less content for film and TV."

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI应用与商业化加速**：Anthropic和Blackstone认为下一个万亿美元AI机遇在于实际部署而非模型本身；AI编码初创公司Emergent以1.3亿美元C轮融资成为独角兽；OpenAI研究员正洽谈成立估值20亿美元的AI药物发现公司。

2. **网络安全与身份管理**：美国指控俄罗斯“防弹”托管服务商参与网络犯罪，受害者损失达6200万美元；新创公司Oak获6000万美元融资，旨在解决AI智能体加剧的身份管理混乱问题。

3. **智能硬件与消费电子**：OpenAI首款硬件设备被曝为无屏可移动音箱；SpaceX前员工融资6500万美元，试图将线束制造从冷战时代升级；Lorde公开批评AI眼镜“不性感”。

4. **地缘科技竞争**：印度投入数十亿美元试图打破中国在智能手机制造领域的主导地位；Spotify为免费用户推出家长管理账户功能。

5. **AI与创意工具**：Reelful利用AI将用户相册自动生成社交媒体短视频；Vint Cerf正计划让AI智能体在开放互联网中自主行动。

**今日最值得关注**：AI正从模型竞赛转向实际部署与行业渗透，同时伴随身份安全、硬件创新与地缘制造格局的深刻变革。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **OpenAI发布GPT-5.6**：该模型成为Microsoft 365 Copilot首选，主打前沿智能与可扩展性，同时推出GPT-5.5生物漏洞赏金计划，并强调ChatGPT成为用户最具雄心工作的合作伙伴。
2. **AI语音质量评估新工具**：Hugging Face推出“Real World VoiceEQ”，用于衡量AI语音的人类质感，提升语音AI的真实性评估标准。
3. **AI代理与数据科学应用**：Hugging Face聚焦“Data for Agents”研究；OpenAI则发布多篇指南，涵盖AI投资管理、数据科学团队及销售团队如何利用ChatGPT Work，以及德国电信用AI重构电信网络。
4. **PyTorch与视觉搜索技术进展**：Hugging Face发布PyTorch性能分析第三部分，聚焦注意力机制优化；Google AI庆祝视觉搜索创新25周年。
5. **国内硬件与AI大会动态**：优可测发布高精度闪测仪，测量精度突破1微米，效率提升3倍；WAIC 2026主论坛下午场议程揭晓。

**最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI正式推出GPT-5.6，并将其深度整合至Microsoft 365 Copilot，标志着前沿AI模型在企业级应用中的规模化落地进入新阶段。
